stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • The capybara is a species of large rodent.
  • It is native to wetlands across South America.
  • The capybara mainly feeds on grasses and aquatic plants.
  • It has an average weight of about 50 kilograms.
stem: The student wants to specify the average weight of the capybara. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: The capybara has an average weight of about 50 kilograms.
The capybara is a large rodent native to South America.
One large rodent found in South American wetlands eats grasses and aquatic plants.
The capybara is a rodent that mainly feeds on grasses and aquatic plants.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The sentence specifies the average weight of the capybara: about 50 kilograms.

Choice B is incorrect. While the sentence provides information about the capybara, it doesn't specify the capybara's average weight. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence describes the diet of a rodent in South American wetlands; it doesn't specify the capybara's average weight. Choice D is incorrect. While the sentence provides information about the capybara, it doesn't specify the capybara's average weight.
